text.title: rag-eval-ab-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Three observable antipatterns when running RAG A/B tests.

antipattern.description: Running all A questions first, then all B questions, without interleaving per question.
antipattern.reason: Temporal drift in LLM API / index / cache state confounds the A/B comparison. Detector: timestamps cluster — A all before B. Fix: enforce r1-interleave-ab.

antipattern.description: Promoting config B because its average latency is lower, without quality metrics.
antipattern.reason: A faster config that returns fewer or less relevant sources is worse for users. Detector: recommendation present without aggregate.metric in {faithfulness, mrr, ndcg}. Fix: enforce r5-quality-over-latency.

antipattern.description: A and B share an embedding cache or vector DB; B benefits from A's warm cache.
antipattern.reason: B's latency looks artificially low. Detector: B latency lower despite no caching improvement in config. Fix: separate indexes + caches per config (r2-isolate-state).
